description_long: This pizzeria is housed in a rustic, single-story building with whitewashed stone walls and a traditional terracotta-tiled roof. The structure features a prominent arched entrance with decorative brickwork and a small bell tower or chimney at the front, adding to its old-world charm. The windows are small and rectangular, framed in dark trim, and the corners of the building are reinforced with contrasting stone blocks. The building sits on a paved stone base, suggesting a sidewalk or patio area, but there is no visible outdoor seating. The roof is uneven and textured, with visible weathering, and the overall appearance is reminiscent of Mediterranean or classic Italian countryside architecture. There are no visible signs or menu boards on the exterior, but the presence of a chimney hints at a wood-fired pizza oven inside.
description: A rustic, whitewashed stone pizzeria with a terracotta-tiled roof, arched entrance, and a small chimney, set on a stone-paved base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched entrance, decorative brickwork, and small bell tower or chimney, with the stone-paved base clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side and rear view highlighting the long, whitewashed walls, small windows, and the full extent of the tiled roof, with no visible entrance.
description_view_2: Rear corner view displaying the back wall, a small arched door, and the textured roof, with minimal architectural detailing.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing another arched door, the length of the building, and the chimney structure at the far end.
